definition: Imperishable; second-highest of five eternal realities; also called Aksharbrahma
Highest divine abode of Bhagwan Swaminarayan (NEVER "divine abode")

Also called Maha-Purush, Mul-Purush, or simply Purush
One with qualities of Akshar; highest spiritual state
Soul / individual self, distinct from physical, subtle, and causal bodies
Supreme Being; Bhagwan Swaminarayan; also called Paramatma, Purushottam
Supreme Being, God; always manifest on earth in human form
Individual soul; one of the five eternal realities; bound by maya
Cosmic being; divine beings governing realms; one of five eternal realities
Cosmic illusion; root cause of ignorance; one of five eternal realities
Primal divine energy; feminine; composed of three gunas
Inner faculty (mind, buddhi, chitt, ahamkar collectively)
Ego; sense of individual existence
Intellect; faculty for discernment and forming convictions
Contemplative faculty; responsible for focusing and storing impressions
Mind; generates thoughts and desires; governs indriyas
Ten sense organs (five of perception, five of action)
Movement of awareness; attention of the mind or sense organs

Righteousness; cosmic/moral order; religious duty

Devotional hymn / song
Liberation; release from cycle of births and deaths
Selfless service to God, guru, or devotees (NEVER just "service")
Holy fellowship; BAPS spiritual community (NEVER "fellowship")
Sacred scripture (NEVER just "scripture" in spiritual context)
Memorised recitation of sacred texts (NEVER "recitation")

Spiritual travels of a guru or sadhu (BAPS spelling; NEVER "vicharan")

Food offering presented to God with devotion
Worship performed with sacred items
Ritual circumambulation of a sacred object clockwise

Double-stranded tulsi bead necklace worn by initiated devotees
U-shaped sacred mark on forehead with sandalwood paste
Auspicious vermilion mark on forehead as sign of devotion
Full prostration as a form of respectful greeting or worship
Highest meditative state; also tomb/memorial of a great saint
Vedic fire sacrifice; ceremonial worship ritual
Individual universe comprising 14 realms
Quality of Prakruti; three types: sattva, rajas, tamas
Purity, clarity, goodness
Activity, passion, restlessness
Darkness, inertia, ignorance
One who has transcended all three gunas of maya
Cosmic intelligence; first principle from Pradhan and Purush
Five subtle elements from which gross elements emerge
Five gross elements (pruthvi, jal, tej, vayu, akash)
Space/ether; one of five gross elements
Earth; one of five gross elements

Cosmic age; four ages: Satya-yug, Treta-yug, Dwapar-yug, Kali-yug
Current age; Age of Darkness; 432,000 human years
Day of Brahma; 4.32 billion human years
Half lifespan of Brahma; 1 x 10^17 human years
Revered title for Bhagwan Swaminarayan
Bhagwan Swaminarayan (always TWO words; NEVER "Shrijimaharaj")
Revered address for the current/previous spiritual successor
Highest order of male sadhu; BAPS renunciant
Male renunciant who wears white robes
True spiritual guide; manifest form of Aksharbrahma on earth
Spiritual teacher who upholds scriptural teachings within a tradition
Ascetic or monk who has renounced worldly life
Person who has renounced all worldly duties; ascetic
Ancient sage or seer; one who has received divine revelations
Ideal devotee of God, referring to the Satpurush
273 recorded divine discourses of Bhagwan Swaminarayan
212 Sanskrit verses by Bhagwan Swaminarayan; code of conduct

18 shastras recording ancient Hindu narratives and teachings
Remembered shastras; include Purans, epics, Dharma-shastras
Esoteric aphorisms expounding essence of Upanishads

Schools of thought from the Upanishads
Non-dual philosophy; ultimate reality is one
Dualistic philosophy; eternal distinction between jiva and God
Philosophy emphasizing enumeration of 25 tattvas
Constant dissolution; daily death of beings

Simple dish of spiced rice and lentils cooked together

Rich delicacy made from sweetened milk and rice with spices
Round sweet made from flour, sugar, and ghee
Indian sweet from condensed milk and sugar

Measure of distance equalling four gaus; approximately 6-7 km
Traditional unit of mass in British India; 25-160 pounds

Rest house for pilgrims and travellers

Vows/spiritual commitments undertaken by devotees
Five fundamental vows for initiated followers
Lifetime eightfold celibacy (precise doctrinal term)
Fourfold discipline: dharma, jnan, vairagya, bhakti
State of being brahmarup (NEVER "Brahmic state")
Collective body of saints (use for group of Swamis)
Ascetic or sadhu; used for impostor/fraudulent religious figures
Standard English for haribhakta (NEVER "haribhakta" in output)
Sanctified by a holy person's presence or use

category: CRITICAL DOCTRINAL DISTINCTIONS (from Master Glossary pp.1-10)
definition: Opposite of dharma; unrighteousness; immorality

category: CRITICAL DOCTRINAL DISTINCTIONS (from Master Glossary pp.1-10)
definition: The physical world; everything material made from the five elements

category: CRITICAL DOCTRINAL DISTINCTIONS (from Master Glossary pp.1-10)
definition: Cosmic forces or deities that control nature and one's senses

category: CRITICAL DOCTRINAL DISTINCTIONS (from Master Glossary pp.1-10)
definition: The inner self; mind, thoughts, feelings, and the jiva's inner world

category: CRITICAL DOCTRINAL DISTINCTIONS (from Master Glossary pp.1-10)
definition: Inner controller; God's power to reside within jiva, ishwar, and control actions

category: CRITICAL DOCTRINAL DISTINCTIONS (from Master Glossary pp.1-10)
definition: Connected; immanent; when used for God, implies inherently existing within

category: CRITICAL DOCTRINAL DISTINCTIONS (from Master Glossary pp.1-10)
definition: Synonymous with maya; false understanding of reality; ignorance

category: CRITICAL DOCTRINAL DISTINCTIONS (from Master Glossary pp.1-10)
definition: Causal body of Virat-Purush and other ishwars

category: CRITICAL DOCTRINAL DISTINCTIONS (from Master Glossary pp.1-10)
definition: Second-highest of five eternal realities (NOT the creator deity Brahma)

category: CRITICAL DOCTRINAL DISTINCTIONS (from Master Glossary pp.1-10)

category: CRITICAL DOCTRINAL DISTINCTIONS (from Master Glossary pp.1-10)

category: CRITICAL DOCTRINAL DISTINCTIONS (from Master Glossary pp.1-10)

category: CRITICAL DOCTRINAL DISTINCTIONS (from Master Glossary pp.1-10)

category: CRITICAL DOCTRINAL DISTINCTIONS (from Master Glossary pp.1-10)
definition: Formless, pure, all-pervading conscious form of Akshar; luminous and eternal

category: CRITICAL DOCTRINAL DISTINCTIONS (from Master Glossary pp.1-10)
definition: Consciousness; the essence of the atma; higher awareness beyond the physical

category: CRITICAL DOCTRINAL DISTINCTIONS (from Master Glossary pp.1-10)
definition: Non-living, inanimate; opposite of chaitanya; without consciousness

category: CRITICAL DOCTRINAL DISTINCTIONS (from Master Glossary pp.1-10)
definition: Inanimate constituents of the world; the eight elements from Pradhan-Prakruti

category: CRITICAL DOCTRINAL DISTINCTIONS (from Master Glossary pp.1-10)
definition: The seer or observer; typically the atma; also God as inner observer

category: CRITICAL DOCTRINAL DISTINCTIONS (from Master Glossary pp.1-10)
definition: Visible; object of perception; the physical body or visible world

definition: Divine power of God to eclipse infinite muktas by his own light

category: CRITICAL DOCTRINAL DISTINCTIONS (from Master Glossary pp.1-10)

definition: State where all mayik influences dissolve through spiritual knowledge

category: CRITICAL DOCTRINAL DISTINCTIONS (from Master Glossary pp.1-10)
definition: Chosen deity; the form of God one feels closest to and worships

category: CRITICAL DOCTRINAL DISTINCTIONS (from Master Glossary pp.1-10)
